headline: Hey Savi and PayPal Launch UK's First Agentic Commerce Platform with In-App Checkout; Debenhams Group Joins as First Retail Adopter
summary: Money20/20 -- Today Hey Savi launched UK's first agentic commerce experience with native checkout powered by PayPal. The app is the first of its kind AI-powered, brand agnostic fashion search platform for women, making it easier for shoppers to find, compare and buy must-have styles without leaving the app. Debenhams Group - including the Group brands Debenhams, Karen Millen, Boohoo and Pretty Little Thing – is the first retailer in the UK to bring end-to-end AI-powered shopping to UK consumers.

content.title: Hey Savi and PayPal Launch UK's First Agentic Commerce Platform with In-App Checkout; Debenhams Group Joins as First Retail Adopter

headline: Michael Burry Bets On PayPal Stock As It Trades Over 80% Down From 2021 Highs
summary: Michael Burry, famed for his role in 'The Big Short,' is making a significant bet on PayPal ($PYPL) stock, which has seen a dramatic decline of over 80% since its 2021 peak. The video also explores PayPal's new partnership as the official peer-to-peer network of the NFL and the 'hot sauce trade' driven by GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic, leading to Tapatio's sale to private equity.

headline: Palantir, XAI, PayPal, and Robinhood stock analysis
summary: Shay Boloor, chief market strategist at Futurum, breaks down Palantir's strong earnings report and future guidance. He also analyzes Elon Musk's decision to merge XAI and SpaceX, and the implications for Tesla shareholders. Finally, Balor offers insights into the struggles of PayPal and the potential of Robinhood as a 'super app'.

headline: Western Union, Visa, PayPal, Meta: why major payments giants are racing to Solana
summary: Kyle Samani, co-founder of Forward, breaks down his latest deal on the main stage at Solana, a minority stake in Henri, a reinsurance RWA yielding 12-13%, funded by a Galaxy debt facility at 3.5%. He makes the case for why Solana has quietly become the dominant chain for institutional payments, tokenized equities, and real world assets. From Western Union and Visa to Meta and PayPal, the biggest names in finance are all building on the same rails. Plus, why he thinks most altcoins are bleeding out, Ethereum L2s are in trouble, and why this bear market might be the best investing opportunity in crypto right now.
